label: Pipeline test

files.raw_diff:
diff --git a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
index a1b2c3d..e5f6g7h 100644
--- a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
+++ b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
@@ -23,10 +23,14 @@
 
 @router.post("/run", status_code=202)
 async def start_run(
     body: PipelineRunRequest,
     background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
 ) -> dict:
     """
     Start a new pipeline run in the background.
     """
+    # Input Validation Guard
+    if not body.diff_text or len(body.diff_text.strip()) < 10:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="diff_text must be at least 10 characters long.")
+        
     orchestrator = PipelineOrchestrator()
     # A temporary orchestration is needed to get the run_id before starting

scenarios.title: Successfully trigger a new pipeline run via API
scenarios.description: Verify that a valid POST request to /api/pipeline/run returns a 202 status and a valid run_id.

steps.assertion: Response status should be 202 and contain a UUID 'run_id'

scenarios.expected_result: Pipeline run is initialized in the background and run_id is returned.

scenarios.title: Trigger pipeline run from UI and verify background task start
scenarios.description: Verify the UI correctly submits the run label and diff text to the /run endpoint.
preconditions: User is on the pipeline execution page

steps.assertion: UI should display a success message or redirect to status page

scenarios.expected_result: The application triggers the background task and provides feedback to the user.

scenarios.title: Verify SSE status stream for an active pipeline run
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/pipeline/status/{run_id} streams the correct lifecycle events (STARTED, DIFF_ANALYZED).
preconditions: A pipeline run has been initiated and run_id is known
scenarios.expected_result: The client receives real-time updates as the pipeline orchestrator processes the diff.

scenarios.title: List completed pipeline reports
scenarios.description: Verify that /api/reports returns a list of completed runs sorted by modification time.
preconditions: At least one pipeline run has completed and saved a JSON report

steps.assertion: Response should be a list containing the recent run_id

scenarios.expected_result: The API returns a JSON array of report summaries.

scenarios.title: Handle missing run_label in pipeline request
scenarios.description: Verify that the API returns a 422 error when the run_label is missing from the request body.

steps.assertion: Response status should be 422 Unprocessable Entity

scenarios.expected_result: Request is rejected due to missing mandatory schema fields.

scenarios.title: Access status for non-existent run_id
scenarios.description: Verify that requesting SSE status for a random UUID returns an empty stream or error.

steps.assertion: Response should not stream valid pipeline events

scenarios.expected_result: The system does not leak data or crash for invalid run IDs.

scenarios.title: Handle empty report directory
scenarios.description: Verify that /api/reports returns an empty list when no report files exist.

steps.assertion: Response should be an empty list []

scenarios.expected_result: API handles the missing directory or empty state gracefully without 500 errors.

scenarios.title: Pipeline run with extremely large diff text
scenarios.description: Verify system stability when processing a multi-megabyte diff file.

steps.assertion: System should accept the request or return a 413 Payload Too Large

scenarios.expected_result: The system processes the request within resource limits or rejects it cleanly.

scenarios.title: XSS injection in run_label field
scenarios.description: Check if a malicious script in the run label is sanitized when viewed in reports.

steps.assertion: UI should render script as plain text, not execute it

scenarios.expected_result: The application prevents stored XSS by sanitizing input or escaping output.

scenarios.title: Validate UUID format for run_id
scenarios.description: Verify that the run_id generated is a valid version 4 UUID as per orchestrator logic.
scenarios.expected_result: Generated IDs follow secure, unpredictable patterns.

error: Invalid format specifier ' 'World'' for object of type 'str'
